file_system_show_account_account_get.description:
description: Show your account information. Unlike show_profile, this includes private information.
model: User
---
success:
{
    "first_name": "string",
    "last_name": "string",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "registered_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
    "last_logged_in": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
    "verified": true
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

file_system_signup_account_post.description:
description: Sign up to create account.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: Your first name

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: Your last name

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Your password.

file_system_delete_account_account_delet.description:
description: Delete your account.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

file_system_update_account_name_account_.description:
description: Update your first or last name in the account profile.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

file_system_logout_auth_logout_post.description:
description: Logout from your account.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

file_system_send_verification_code_send_.description:
description: Send account verification code to your email address.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

file_system_verify_account_verify_accoun.description:
description: Verify your account using the verification code sent to your email address.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

parameters.name: verification_code
parameters.description: The verification code sent to your email address.

file_system_send_password_reset_code_sen.description:
description: Send password reset code to your email address.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

file_system_reset_password_reset_passwor.description:
description: Reset your password using the password reset code sent to your email address.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

parameters.name: password_reset_code
parameters.description: The password reset code sent to your email address.

parameters.name: new_password
parameters.description: Your new password.

file_system_show_profile_profile_get.description:
description: Show public profile information of a user.
model: User
---
success:
{
    "first_name": "string",
    "last_name": "string",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "registered_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00"
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email of the person you want to see the profile information of.

file_system_create_directory_directory_p.description:
description: Create a directory if it does not exist, optionally recursively.
model: Directory
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: If True, it will create all parent directories recursively if they don't exist.

parameters.name: allow_if_exists
parameters.description: If True, it will not raise an error if the directory already exists.

file_system_delete_directory_directory_d.description:
description: Delete a directory with its sub-directories and files.
model: Directory
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

file_system_show_directory_directory_get.description:
description: Show a list of files and/or sub-directories, optionally recursively, in a directory.
model: Directory
---
success:
[
    "string"
]
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: substring
parameters.description: If passed, only files and sub-directories containing the given substring (ignoring case) will be shown.

parameters.name: entry_type
parameters.description: Whether to show all files and sub-directories, only files, or only sub-directories.
constraints: must be one of: [all, files, directories]

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Whether to show files recursively.

file_system_directory_exists_directory_e.description:
description: Check if a directory exists.
model: Directory
---
success:
{"exists": true}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

file_system_show_file_file_get.description:
description: Show a file's content and other details, if it exists.
model: File
---
success:
{
    "file_id": 1,
    "path": "string",
    "content": "string",
    "created_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
    "updated_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00"
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path of the file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path of the file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The content of the file.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path of the file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The updated content of the file.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path of the file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

file_system_file_exists_file_exists_get.description:
description: Check if a file exists.
model: File
---
success:
{"exists": true}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path of the file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: source_file_path
parameters.description: Path of the source file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: destination_file_path
parameters.description: Path of the destination file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists.

parameters.name: retain_dates
parameters.description: Whether the copied file should retain the original file's created and updated dates.

parameters.name: source_file_path
parameters.description: Path of the source file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: destination_file_path
parameters.description: Path of the destination file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists.

parameters.name: retain_dates
parameters.description: Whether the moved file should retain the original file's created and updated dates.

parameters.name: source_directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the source directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: destination_directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the destination directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the directory if it already exists.

parameters.name: retain_dates
parameters.description: Whether the files in the copied directory should retain the created and updated dates of the files in the original directory.

parameters.name: source_directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the source directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: destination_directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the destination directory. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the directory if it already exists.

parameters.name: retain_dates
parameters.description: Whether the files in the moved directory should retain the created and updated dates of the files in the original directory.

parameters.name: directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the directory to compress. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

parameters.name: compressed_file_path
parameters.description: Path of the compressed file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'. If passed, must have an extension: zip or tar. If not passed, it'll be stored as a zip file with the base name of directory_path in directory_path's parent directory.

parameters.name: delete_directory
parameters.description: Whether to delete the directory after compression.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the compressed file if it already exists.

parameters.name: compressed_file_path
parameters.description: Path of the compressed file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'. Must have an extension: zip or tar.

parameters.name: decompressed_directory_path
parameters.description: Path of the directory to save decompressed files. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'. If not passed, it'll be saved in the same directory as the compressed file.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the decompressed directory if it already exists.

parameters.name: retain_dates
parameters.description: Whether the decompressed files should retain the created and updated dates of the files in the compressed file.

parameters.name: delete_compressed_file
parameters.description: Whether to delete the compressed file after decompression.
